item.title: Powerful
item.description: The idea that a women can be as powerful as a man is something that our society can't deal with. But I'm as powerful as a man and it drives them crazy. - AOC

item.dc:creator: UmlimitedTrickPony

item.title: In this Dump: Derek's Haircut, British People, and Me, Bitching
item.description:
I don't normally write stuff inbetween memes in my dumps.
But I'm pissed off, so here we are.
For one, I've been sick for 10 days now, with some stupid respiratory bug that JUST. WON'T. GO. AWAY.
And the fucking cough hasn't let me sleep more than 3 hours/night for the last however many days.
So basically I'm dead on my feet right about now and I hate it and I honestly just want to cry.
And then I made a serious post, a political post, and people actually read it, which was amazing because it's about a really fucking important thing.
And when it was almost at FP, that fucking rightwing bot army guy swooped in and gave it like 17 downvotes in 10 minutes.
So now I'm even more pissed.
Because who the FUCK does he think he is?
And I'm also really, really tired, literally and metaphorically.
So here we are.
Not quite in hell, though. Yet.
Also, there's this guy, so we've got that going for us.
Oh shut up.
item.dc:creator: ParallelParkingInABurka

item.dc:creator: Pentjotal

item.title: Happy International Women's Day
item.description:
Stephanie Louise Kwolek July 31, 1923 – June 18, 2014) was a Polish-American chemist who is known for inventing Kevlar. Her career at the DuPont company spanned more than 40 years. She discovered the first of a family of synthetic fibers of exceptional strength and stiffness: poly-paraphenylene terephthalamide.
Lizzie Magie Elizabeth J. Magie Phillips was an American game designer, writer, feminist, and Georgist. She invented The Landlord's Game, the precursor to Monopoly, to illustrate teachings of the progressive era economist Henry George.
Mary Elizabeth Anderson was an American real estate developer, rancher, viticulturist, and inventor of the windshield wiper blade. On November 10, 1903 Anderson was granted her first patent for an automatic car window cleaning device controlled from inside the car, called the windshield wiper.
Rosalind Franklin The discovery of the DNA double helix is often attributed to James Watson and Francis Click, who won the Nobel Prize for the discovery in 1962. However, they weren’t actually the ones who made the discovery. The first person to capture the photographic image using x-ray diffraction was British biophysicist Rosalind Franklin. Though her two competitors stole credit for the discovery long ago, Rosalind has more recently begun to receive credit for her contributions.
Judith Love Cohen (August 16, 1933 – July 25, 2016) was an American aerospace engineer. She was an electrical engineer on the Minuteman missile, the science ground station for the Hubble Space Telescope, the Tracking and Data Relay Satellite, and the Apollo Space Program.[2] In particular, her work on the Abort-Guidance System is credited with helping save Apollo 13. After her retirement from engineering, she founded a children's multimedia publishing company,[3] eventually publishing more than 20 titles before her death in 2016. She was the mother of computer scientist and engineer Neil Siegel and actor-musician Jack Black.
item.dc:creator: DropDrop

item.title: Saturday WHOLESOME dump to help you with your mental health #419
item.description:
1. Such a nice guy
2
3. 10 million years evolutionary history of friendship between men summarized in 26 seconds (you know they were doin this shit in 300 000 B.C.)
4. ONE OF US
5. Awesome random interaction
6. We don't deserve these boys
8. Nom, nom nom
9. You are getting taken care of! Whether you like it or not :D
10. Brave girl
11. I am elephant
13. Meet the 26-year-old African spurred tortoise, Bon-Chan and his kind owner Mitani-san in Tsukishima.
14. I haven't implemented a puppy tax in a log time. Sorry gotta hit you with it today!
15. Today I wanted to share with you that I started reading this awesome book called Blood, Sweat & Pixels. It's about the history of various indie and mainstream games and the struggle of the devs to get them done. I have to say it's very inspiring and it helps me on my own journey as a game dev. To be honest, I've been working on the same game for almost 2 years now and it can get a bit frustrating. Imagine having to hold your focus on one thing for so long (while juggling everyday life). However this book inspired me and helped me re-start my passion. Anyway, if you want to support the game, a simple wishlist would mean the world to me. Please check it out on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. 1. This here is a plushie duck that I'm hoping a lot of players will develop affection for. P.S. 2 HAVE A GREAT AND SUNNY WEEKEND, FRIENDOS!

item.title: They're eating the pets ! They're eating the cats ! I'm not sure, look :
item.description:
2 :: Finally eating
Rooooky !
4 :: what is that foam, mate ??
Good job my cat !
6 :: A good catcher cat
Mesmerizing cat pull-up
8 :: Mother cat introduces her newborn kitten to owner's baby...
Psychologist: get a cat. Cats relieve stress. Me:
10 :: So brave
Last slap was personal though
12 :: Dual purpose Sphynx
Cat's grand entrance.
14 :: It's a Navy SEAL
It's so nice to lie there and watch the fish
16 :: That's amore
The best jump i've ever seen
18 :: When You Walk In On Your Cat Summoning Satan
why does your tail look like that ???
20 :: Yep, he's a call center agent.
Сat who knows his own worth
22 :: an evil laugh suits this cat perfectly
Almost all cats like to play with water
24 :: Funnnnny
A cat is indispensable in the home
26 :: I Bought a Tire to use as cat house
He works for the circus
28 :: monday morning vibes
No, don't lock me up
30 :: No pain no gain -- This cat can start the machine
she's like 'Yo! what's good'
32 :: He needs to be fed right away
Give me my shrimp
34 :: Feline, Best in Acting Awardee!
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.title: Viper – Doomsday (2008)
item.description:
Stuntwoman and actress Lee-Anne Liebenberg portrays Viper, a commander from a society of savages in a post-apocalyptic Scotland
Set 27 years after Scotland was quarantined due to a virus, the film follows a force led by Eden Sinclair (Rhona Mitra) that enters the forbidden zone to find a cure
This UK sci fi by Neil Marshall (Dog Soldiers, The Descent) unfortunately blends far more inspirations than it can handle, trading coherency and credibility for fast-cut spectacle
Its great cast (including Bob Hoskins, Malcolm McDowell, Alexander Siddig, Craig Conway, Sean Pertwee above, etc) has little to work with, yet there are still interesting elements
One of these is obviously the fell and styled up warrior who would have made the ancient Picts proud, or at least Sawney Bean
Despite a limited role was Viper elevated to the icon of the flick and is on many posters and covers
It is the best known acting role of Lee-Anne Liebenberg. As a stuntwoman she has credits as performer/double, coordinator, fight choreographer, and precision driver
Among the films she worked on are Avengers: Age of Ultron, Chappie, and District 9
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: What Happened Today #35
item.description: Tariff threats resurface as Ukraine loses access to commercial satellites, Poland gears up for nuclear access, and the State Department plans to deploy AI to scan foreign students—global power is shifting. #resist #GovBriefToday LINKS 1 - https://www.cnn.com/2025/03/07/business/tariffs-trump-canada/index.html 2 - https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5182126-trump-federal-workers-firing-lawsuit/ 3 - https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/us/several-top-career-officials-ousted-at-justice-department/ar-AA1Au1JA 4 - https://www.politico.eu/article/donald-tusk-plan-train-poland-men-military-service-russia/ 5 - https://abcnews.go.com/International/russia-conducts-massive-aerial-attack-ukraine-overnight/story?id=119543077 6 - https://www.propublica.org/article/hud-considers-crypto-blockchain-stablecoin-housing-urban-development 7 - https://gizmodo.com/state-department-will-use-ai-to-search-for-pro-hamas-students-to-deport-2000573143 8 - https://www.cbsnews.com/news/tsa-homeland-security-collective-bargaining-agreement-union/ 9 - https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/07/usda-eliminates-two-food-safety-advisory-committees.html 10 - https://www.npr.org/2025/03/07/nx-s1-5321326/trump-administration-columbia-university-400-million-cancelled 11 - https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/education/2025/03/07/trump-student-loan-relief-forgiveness-executive-order/81973679007/ 12 - https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/mar/07/cdc-study-vaccines-autism 13 - https://wtop.com/government/2025/03/new-epa-guidance-says-spending-items-greater-than-50000-must-get-approval-from-doge/ 14 - https://www.washingtonian.com/2025/03/07/william-treanor-georgetown-law-ed-martin-first-amendment/ 15 - https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/44155171/president-donald-trump-task-force-2026-world-cup 16 - https://apnews.com/article/trump-louisiana-cancer-denka-chloroprene-epa-lawsuit-89b1f870816f65370eeb75ff269e9c0e 17 - https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2025/03/07/trump-russia-sanctions-threat/ FIGHTING BACK - https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/mar/07/montana-anti-trans-bills-defeated Archive - https://govbrief.today

item.title: Protect your pets
item.description: I got the colors backwards. Thanks for telling me! View the Jackson Galaxy video here https://youtu.be/9phPG2uqFCQ?feature=shared Biggest risks: Raw poultry Raw milk Wild birds Bird droppings Consider: Not feeding Raw or freeze dried poultry Not feeding Raw milk Keeping cats indoors Wiping shoes with clorox wipes when coming indoors. Protect your pets Protect your people from transmission with reservoir populations. Edit, ty for accolades. Please share the PSA. We can reduce the risk of more species jumping by reducing exposure. Edit: Someone in the comments @onetimepost9 expressed that they had trouble finding the source. Here's a couple of links that are in the source above. https://www.cdc.gov/bird-flu/situation-summary/data-map-commercial.html https://www.fda.gov/safety/recalls-market-withdrawals-safety-alerts If you need more help finding the links in the source, you can expand the details in the video by tapping on "more."
item.dc:creator: MurderKittyComics

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: The Truth! :)
item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: History dump pt1: People being people throughout history (and cat tax)
item.description:
1 Clearing a bunch of history memes from my phone. These reminds me of the saying 'The more things change, the more they stay the same'. Also, Weird Al is a goddamn treasure.
2
3 ACAB, and turtles are awesome.
4
5 Someone: "People back then didn't care about their animals as we do today." Me: [points to this] Exhibit A
6 Exhibit B I shared this before, but I love it so much. Catowners have changed very little since the furry freeloaders decided to move in with us and we started putting bows on them.
7 Exhibit C
8 Exhibit D
9 Exhibit E
10 Bureaucracy hasn't changed much.
11
12 I think I'm gonna use one of these excuses the next time I wanna get out of a social obligation. "Sorry, can't come to your candle sales event disguised as low-key dinner party. I'm embalming my brother then."
13 Something something 'Last of us' reference, idk...
14
15 19th century cosplay.
16
17 Ancient Sonic.
18
19 Modern day military personnel writing messages on missils.
20
21 Most epic music battle ever. Eat your heart out, Eminem.
22 This reminded me of the time I made a caricature in my textbook of our philosophy teacher, giving it the title "Goldfish" because she kinda looked like a goldfish. Decades later, I found my mom's history textbook with a caricature of a very frog-like teacher, titled "the Frog".
23 Look, you can have me actually listening while I doodle, or you can stop me from doodling and I won't be able to listen.
24
25 Look at that drip.
26
27
28 We can't have a history dump without at least one Ea-Nasir reference. I'm sorry, I don't make the rules, that's just the way it is.
29
30 Haha, yeah, they were so silly in the early 1900s. Nowadays, we would never post pics of bugs looking like plants or flowers looking like animals...
31
32 Little Onfim, being bored with his homework and drawing pictures of himself and his friend as warriors. A modern kid could've done this. Google 'Onfim Novgorod' for more details and better pics, this one uploaded like crap for some reason. I swear, it has better resolution in my gallery.
33 Ye olde screengrabs.
34 Teenage girls, being little chaos gremlins through history.
35
36 I feel you, Edith Anne. I hated embroidery too.
37 Ol' Horace would've ruled YouTube.
38 Frog memes are eternal.
39
40 Humans have had an urge for documenting our experiences since the first person decided to paint an animal hunt on a cave wall over 30000 years ago.
41 Been there....
42
43 "Apoxpalon, pick up your doll." "Mom! It's not a 'doll', it's an action figure. Sweet Hunraqan, don't you know anything?"
44 "I have several concubines, but you went into another man's house, so bye-bye."
45 Neolithic gummy bear.
46
47 I'm starting to think crinolines and bustles were about avoiding getting groped by men.
48 Me, making sure my oatmeal cookies don't get overbaked.
49 Cat tax: Miss Charlotta, probably plotting her revenge. Charlie refuses to walk in snow, so in wintertime she can be let outside without a leash. But enough snow has thawed, so today I put her harness on for the first time this year. She wasn't amused at first, but caved quickly when she realised she wouldn't be let out without it.
50 Nighty night, Charlie. When summer is here you can nap outside again.
item.dc:creator: Kittensandyarns
